reply:
[[BLOCK:slide33_shape1]]
AICc (A corrected version of Akaike’s Information Criterion, imposing a greater penalty for additional parameters in small sample sizes) applies a stricter penalty for the inclusion of additional parameters.  
AICc = AIC + (2K(K+1)/(n-k-1)), where n represents the sample size and k denotes the number of parameters.  
Burnham and Anderson (2002) advocate for the use of AICc, particularly when n is small and k is large.  
As n increases, AICc converges to AIC.  
AICc is recommended for use irrespective of the values of n and k.
